task_completion.description: Verify that the five specified task tiddlers have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the titles of the five tasks ('Migrate Database to PostgreSQL 16', 'Update API Documentation', 'Fix Authentication Timeout Bug', 'Design New Dashboard Mockups', 'Set Up CI-CD Pipeline for Staging') in the 'Recent' tab of the sidebar or open in the main story river.

task_completion.description: Verify that the task tiddlers are tagged with 'Sprint-Tasks'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Sprint-Tasks' tag pill on the task tiddlers that are visible in the story river.

task_completion.description: Verify that the custom fields (priority, assignee, status, due-date) are correctly assigned to the task tiddlers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom of the task tiddlers (in view mode) or in the fields section (in edit mode) to see the custom fields 'priority', 'assignee', 'status', and 'due-date' populated with the correct values according to the task description.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Sprint Board' tiddler is created and tagged 'Sprint-Tasks'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a tiddler named 'Sprint Board' open in the story river or listed in the sidebar, and check that it has the 'Sprint-Tasks' tag pill.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Sprint Board' tiddler contains the correct headings and list widgets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In edit mode, look for headings 'Open Tasks' and 'High Priority' followed by `<$list>` widgets filtering by `status[open]` and `priority[high]`. In view mode, verify that the 'Open Tasks' section lists the 3 open tasks and the 'High Priority' section lists the 2 high priority tasks.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the TiddlyWiki graphical user interface to create the tiddlers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the TiddlyWiki interface, such as the tiddler editor, sidebar tabs, and field input boxes, rather than a terminal or code editor.

integrity.description: Verify that the tiddlers and fields are created as native TiddlyWiki elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The custom fields should be entered in the dedicated field input area of the tiddler editor, not just typed as plain text in the tiddler body.
